titleStmt.title: SII 25.43: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 43. (A.R. No. 43 of 1908.) KAḺUGUMALAI, MELUR TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a slab in the Kośakkuḍi street.

This record refers to an expedition of the Pāṇḍya army against Śaḍaiyaṉ-Karunandaṉ of Malai-nāḍu, in which the fort of Arivāyūr was destroyed. This Śaḍaiyaṉ-Karunandaṉ is identical with the Āy chieftain whose records are found in the northern part of the Travancore region. He may have originally been a Pāṇḍya vassal as indicated by the surname Śaḍaiyaṉ and may have subsequently rebelled against the Pāṇḍya in conjunction with the Vēṇāḍu ruler of this period, which action was probably the cause of the expedition referred to in the present inscription. Two soldiers of the battalion commanded by the Pāṇḍya subordinate Eṭṭi-Maṇṇaṉ alias Maṅgala-Ēṉādi of Perunechchuṟam, named Viṉaiyantoḻu-śūraṉ of Pūndaṉmali in Toṇḍai-nāḍu and Śāttaṉakkaṉ of Pēreyiṟkuḍi lost their lives in this fight, and in commemoration of their services, some land valued at 20 kaḻañju of gold was given apparently by this Ēṉādi. This endowment, which was made over to the sabhai of Kumaramaṅgalam, may have been in the nature of an udirappaṭṭi.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 31.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrī komāṟañcaṭaiyaṟ
ku yāṇṭu Irupattu
mūṉṟu Avvāṇṭu ma
lai nāṭṭu caṭaiyaṅ
karu nantaṉār meṟpaṭai po
y Ariviūrk koṭṭai
Aḻittu naṉṟu cey
tu paṭṭār perunec
cuṟattu Eṭṭi maṇ
ṇaṉāyiṉa maṅkala E
ṉāti Uḷviṭṭuk koyi
ṟ cevakariruvar . Avaka
ḷi loruvaṉ ṟoṇṭai
nāṭṭu pūntaṇmali vi
ṉaiyan toḻucūraṉ Oruva
ṉai pereyiṟkuṭic cāt
taṉakkaṉ . Ivviruvaraiyu
ñ cārtti Irupatiṉ kaḻa
ñcu poṉ peṟum pūmi A
ṭṭikkuṭuttāṉ kummaṇa
maṅkalattu capaiyārkku
kalapaṭṭama
Eṉāti .
Digital edition of SII 25.43 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
